race.name: Craftsbury Marathon
race.tagline: The Northeast Kingdom's longest day on snow — forty-five kilometers of Vermont's fiercest winter through America's most storied ski center.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple feed stations with warm beverages and food

climate.description: Craftsbury Common sits in Vermont's coldest corner. January-February race dates deliver reliable cold, typically -18°C to -5°C. The Northeast Kingdom receives consistent natural snowfall supplemented by snowmaking at the Craftsbury Outdoor Center.
challenges: Bitter cold — Northeast Kingdom is Vermont's icebox
Short daylight window in late January
Variable snow conditions between shaded forest and open farmland

course.primary: Multi-lap course radiating from the Craftsbury Outdoor Center through rolling Vermont farmland and mixed hardwood forest. Steady climbing with no flat respite — the terrain never lets you rest.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails through forest and open meadow terrain
course.grooming: Craftsbury Outdoor Center standard — meticulous grooming with snowmaking backup
features: Craftsbury Outdoor Center — a premier US Nordic training facility
Rolling Vermont farmland with panoramic views
The East's largest Nordic ski marathon
Training ground for US Biathlon and ski team members

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Excellent grooming from a world-class training center and beautiful Vermont scenery. Limited by small field size and remote location in the Northeast Kingdom.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: TIER 3 — a boutique marathon with outsized character

history.summary: Founded in 1981 at the Craftsbury Outdoor Center, the Craftsbury Marathon has grown into the largest Nordic ski marathon in the eastern United States. The Craftsbury Outdoor Center — established in 1976 — is one of America's premier cross-country ski training facilities, home to the Green Racing Project elite team. The marathon leverages this infrastructure to deliver a well-groomed course through Vermont's remote and beautiful Northeast Kingdom.
notable_facts: The largest Nordic ski marathon in the eastern United States
Hosted at the Craftsbury Outdoor Center, a US elite training facility
The Green Racing Project elite team trains on the same trails year-round
Craftsbury Common is one of the coldest inhabited places in Vermont
The Center's snowmaking ensures coverage even in lean snow years
Both classic and freestyle events offered

youtube_data.search_query: Craftsbury Marathon cross country ski race Craftsbury Common

videos.title: GRP RUN - Meet the Team!
videos.description:
That's right- The Craftsbury Green Racing Project now boasts a professional running contingent. Meet the members of the inaugural GRP Run team, which includes post-collegiate racers from mid-distance to ultramarathon. Check out https://www.greenracingproject.com/run to learn more about the team including its coaches and its relationship with the Craftsbury Outdoor Center.

Follow our channel for updates as we post more interviews and content! 

Join Craftsbury online today to support us and our mission of lifelong sports, sustainability and stewardship:  http://craftsbury.com/membership

videos.title: Craftsbury hosts USSA Nordic races
videos.description: Some of the Top Nordic skiers are at the Craftsbury Outdoor Center this week for the USSA Nordic SuperTour Finals

videos.title: A Day at Craftsbury
videos.description:
We spent a weekend in Craftsbury, which would never be complete without a day cross-country skiing at the Craftsbury Outdoor Center. I grew up skiing here, and it had been a while since I'd been back.  It was my first time skiing there since their major expansions. Impeccable grooming, amazing conditions the day after a snowstorm and a great time with old friends. It was pretty cold though.  
#nordicskiing #xcskiing #craftsbury
Music: www.bensound.com

videos.title: The Craftsbury Marathon 2012
videos.description: The biggest Cross Country Skiing event in Vermont is the annual Craftsbury Marathon. It attracts hundreds of skiers. Unfortunately the winter of 2011-2012 did not bring a lot of snow. The organizers had to reduce the length of the 25 km and 50 km courses due to insufficient snow. Instead, the race was conducted over a shorter course which the contestants had to ski, once, twice or three times. Still, the course did afford some picturesque countryside and the weather on the day of the race, January 28th, was nice. Film with Canon DSLRs

videos.curation_reason: Recent POV vlog with pre-race and post-race commentary specifically about the Craftsbury Marathon.

videos.title: The 35th Craftsbury Marathon
videos.description: More than 400 participants in the 12.5 and 25k marathons at the Craftsbury Sports center in Vermont's Northeast Kingdom
videos.curation_reason: Race coverage specifically of the Craftsbury Marathon showing the event atmosphere and participants.

quotes.text: It was really snowy as you can see and it was really hectic out there like honestly I was skiing like in pretty deep powder to try and pass people to make sure I would move on to the final and so it was like definitely just like a pretty challenging day but it was like pretty fun out there.

This race course in the snow falling snow and whatnot definitely favored people who could get off the line pretty quickly, so I was definitely focused on that trying to make sure that I was like getting off the line really fast once I heard that gun go off.
